properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: The ISO 4217 code, for display and serialization.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Construct an amount from a whole number of minor units.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Construct from major and minor units, e.g. `from_major(12, 34, EUR)`
is 12.34 EUR. `minor` is taken modulo 100 only in the sense that the
caller is expected to pass 0..99; this helper is a convenience for
fixtures and does no validation, matching how a literal amount is
written by hand.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Zero in a given currency, the additive identity for `add`.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Add two amounts of the *same* currency. Mixing currencies is a
typed error, never an implicit conversion: claimdesk has no FX
table and inventing one would hide a real data problem.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Subtract `b` from `a` (same currency). The result may be negative.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Sum a list of amounts, all required to share `currency`. An empty
list sums to `zero(currency)`. Folding through `Result` means a
single mismatched entry aborts the whole total with a clear error.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: True when two amounts are equal in both magnitude and currency.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Ordering helper restricted to a single currency: -1 if a < b, 0 if
equal, 1 if a > b. Comparing across currencies is meaningless, so it
is a typed error rather than a number.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Render as a human-readable amount with two decimals and the ISO
code, e.g. `1234.56 EUR` or `-7.05 USD`. Pure integer arithmetic
keeps it byte-identical on both backends; no Float ever appears.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Total of a list of expense lines, all required to share `currency`.
Reuses money.sum, so a line in the wrong currency is a typed error,
not a silently wrong total.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Mint an authorization for an approved claim. The only way to obtain
a `DisbursementAuthorization`; from here the linear discipline
guarantees the returned token is consumed exactly once.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Consume an authorization, discharging the linear obligation and
returning a receipt. `consume` makes this the token's terminal
owner: after `settle_authorization(authz)` the caller no longer
holds `authz`, and any attempt to use it again is a compile error.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Open a fresh claim in Draft. The lifecycle-only fields start
neutral; the transitions populate them in order.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Draft -> Submitted. The employee submits the claim; `at` is the
submission tick (a fixed value in Phase 1 for determinism).

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Submitted -> UnderReview. A reviewer picks the case up and is
recorded on the claim.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
UnderReview -> Approved. The reviewer approves; the decider is
recorded. Only a claim that is actually under review can be
approved.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
UnderReview -> Rejected. The reviewer rejects; the decider is
recorded. Rejected is terminal.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Approved -> Settled. Records the payment by consuming a linear
DisbursementAuthorization (spent exactly once) and returns the
settled claim together with the immutable receipt. Only an Approved
claim can be settled, and only with a fresh authorization.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
The approved total. Available only on an Approved claim: there is
no approved amount to read in any earlier state, and the type
system enforces that.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: The decider of record, for minting the authorization.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Who is being paid. Available only once the claim is settled.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Terminal sink for a settled claim: consumes the linear value,
discharging its obligation. A settled claim is the end of the
protocol; archiving it is how the lifecycle is closed out. In a
later phase this is where the ledger record is written.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Who rejected the claim. Available only on a Rejected claim.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Terminal sink for a rejected claim. Like `archive` on a settled
claim, it consumes the linear value so a rejected claim cannot be
silently abandoned.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: A one-line rendering of an outcome, for reports.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: A one-line rendering of a decision, for reports.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Pair a label with an outcome.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
An empty tally, the fold's seed. `seed` is an empty `List<T>` whose
only job is to fix the reason type `T` at the call site: a generic
function with no value parameter cannot have its type argument
inferred on the Wasm backend (see the BACKEND NOTE in this module),
so the seed list carries it explicitly.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Fold one outcome (already reduced to its reason text) into a tally.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Join a list of reasons into one "; "-separated line. Pure string
folding, identical on both backends.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Reduce a tally to a `Decision`: any fail rejects, otherwise any warn
needs info, otherwise approve for the validated total.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Run a policy over a claim's lines. `total` is the claim's validated
total in its own currency; the currency-consistency rule is expected
to be in `rules` so a mixed-currency claim is caught explicitly
rather than trusting a total that was seeded in the wrong currency.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: One lowercase hex character for a nibble in 0..15.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Encode a byte list as a lowercase hex String, two chars per byte.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Rotate a 32-bit word right by `n` bits. `x` is assumed already
masked to 32 bits; `(x << (32 - n))` can reach 2^63 only if x had
high bits, so the whole expression is re-masked to 32 bits.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Logical right shift of an already-masked 32-bit word.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
The 64 round constants (first 32 bits of the fractional parts of
the cube roots of the first 64 primes).

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Read one 32-bit big-endian word from `block` starting at byte `i`.
Each byte is in 0..255; the top byte is masked to 8 bits before
the 24-bit shift so the product stays well under i64.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Build the 64-word message schedule for one 64-byte block.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
One block of the compression function. Returns the updated state
(the eight working words added back into the incoming state).

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
SHA padding: append 0x80, then zeros, then the 64-bit big-endian
bit length, to a multiple of 64 bytes. Returns a fresh byte list.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Run the full hash over `message` starting from `iv`, returning the
eight 32-bit state words.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Serialize `count` state words as big-endian bytes (4 each).

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
SHA-256 digest of `message` as 32 raw bytes.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
SHA-256 digest of `message` as a 64-character lowercase hex String.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: SHA-256 of a String's UTF-8 bytes, as lowercase hex.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
SHA-224 digest of `message` as 28 raw bytes (the first seven
state words; the eighth is dropped).
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
SHA-224 digest of `message` as a 56-character lowercase hex String.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: SHA-224 of a String's UTF-8 bytes, as lowercase hex.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Normalize a key to exactly 64 bytes: hash it if it is longer than
the block, then zero-pad to the block size.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: XOR every byte of a 64-byte block with a constant pad byte.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Concatenate two byte lists into a fresh list.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
HMAC-SHA256 of `message` under `key`, as 32 raw bytes.
BYTE CONTRACT: each element of `key` and `message` must be in
0..255. Out-of-range elements are silently masked to 8 bits
(`& 0xFF`: 256 -> 0, -1 -> 255), with no error, identically on
both backends. For text, prefer the `_utf8` wrapper or
`String.bytes()`, which are always in range.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
HMAC-SHA256 of `message` under `key`, as lowercase hex.
BYTE CONTRACT: each element of `key` and `message` must be in
0..255; out-of-range elements are silently masked to 8 bits
(`& 0xFF`), with no error, identically on both backends.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
HMAC-SHA256 over the UTF-8 bytes of a String key and message, as
lowercase hex.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Compare two byte lists in constant time, returning whether they are
equal. Differing lengths return false immediately (a tag's length
is not secret). For equal lengths, every byte pair is XORed and the
differences are OR-accumulated across the WHOLE list with no
short-circuit, so the running time depends only on the length, not
on where (or whether) the two lists first differ.

BYTE CONTRACT: each element of `a` and `b` should be in 0..255, as
produced by the hash functions here or by `String.bytes()`. Out-of-
range elements are masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`) before comparison,
identically on both backends, so e.g. 256 and 0 compare equal.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Compare two strings for equality in constant time over their UTF-8
bytes. Intended for tags carried as text: two HMAC hex digests, two
one-time codes, two base64 tokens. Delegates to `bytes_equal` after
`.bytes()`; the time depends only on the byte length, not on the
position of the first difference.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Build a lifecycle event. Every field is public; the IBAN-derived
fields must already be the masked / declassified forms from the
caller, never the raw secret.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Canonical serialization of an event: a single deterministic line
with a fixed field order and separator. The chain MAC is computed
over this exact string, so the serialization IS the tamper surface:
changing any field changes the bytes hashed.

Built in two halves and joined: the Wasm backend caps a single
FormatStr at 8 value parts, and this row has nine fields, so the
interpolation is split rather than widened. The bytes are identical
on both backends.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
The chain MAC for one entry: HMAC over (prev_chain || "|" || body)
under the chain key. capa_hash takes (key, message) in that order.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
One chained line: serialize the event, fold in the previous chain
value, and attach the new chain MAC.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Build the full chained ledger from a list of events. Each line's
chain MAC threads into the next, anchoring the sequence to GENESIS.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Chain a segment of events onto an existing chain value. Threads each
line's MAC into the next and returns the trailing chain value, so a
caller can persist the ledger incrementally (one claim at a time)
while keeping a single, unbroken chain anchored to GENESIS. The
genesis anchor for the first segment is the public `GENESIS` constant.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
True iff a stored MAC matches the recomputed one, compared in
constant time. This wrapper is itself marked @constant_time(): the
MAC bytes are not branched on (the only branch, on length, is over
public tag length, which leaks nothing). The Bool it returns is the
function's RESULT, not a control-flow decision.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Verify the integrity of a chain of ledger lines. Walks from GENESIS,
recomputes each link's MAC over the stored body and the previous
chain value, and returns false on the first mismatch. The per-link
MAC equality is constant-time; the loop control (advancing on a
matched, public chain value) is ordinary. Tampering with any stored
body or MAC makes this return false.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Render one ledger line to its on-disk text form.

properties.name: capa:doc
capa:doc
properties.value:
Persist the ledger. The content is public by construction (every
LedgerEvent field is public and the IBAN-derived fields were masked /
declassified upstream); if any @secret value reached it, this
Fs.write would be an information-flow violation. `fs` is expected to
be attenuated to the output directory by the caller (fs.restrict_to).

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
A dialect with an arbitrary single-character delimiter. A
delimiter that is not exactly one character, or that is the
quote character itself, is a programming error the parser
rejects with `BadDialect` rather than misbehaving silently.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
A human-readable, backend-identical rendering of an error.
Positions are 1-based record numbers, the unit an operator
reasons about.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Parse CSV text with the default comma dialect.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Parse CSV text with an explicit dialect.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Parse CSV text and take its first record as the header.
Empty input yields an empty header and no rows.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Parse with an explicit dialect, first record as the header.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Build a header view from an already-parsed Table. The first
record becomes the columns; the rest become the data rows.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: The number of data records (header excluded).

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: The zero-based index of a column, or None if absent.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Whether the header declares a column by this name.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
The value of `column` in data record `row_index`
(zero-based, header excluded). Errors with `NoSuchColumn`
when the header has no such column, with `NoSuchRow` when
there is no data record at `row_index`, and with `ShortRow`
when the record exists but has fewer fields than the
column's index.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Serialise a Table to CSV text with the comma dialect.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Serialise a Table to CSV text with an explicit dialect.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Serialise a single list of fields to one CSV record (no
trailing newline). Useful for streaming a header line or
appending rows one at a time.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Serialise a single record with an explicit delimiter.
